paragraphs: You have a Roman toe form with medium-width forefoot and a very wide heel. A mixed profile; forefoot and heel require different fits.

Beyond the obvious: Given your Roman toes, rather avoid very pointy shoes. These might squeeze your toe tips, even if the width of the shoe fits you. Given your particularly long arch, the ball of your foot may be pushed into the toe box. A squeezed forefoot may be caused by this instead of actual shoe width, so look for rather short toe boxes. Additionally your instep is rather high, so an adjustable closure is preferable to actually get into the shoe. Ideally double velcro or laces, you may struggle getting into slippers if adequately downsized. Your deep heel projects further back than most, so a deeper, more sculpted heel cup fits naturally.
interpretation.title: Your Foot Shape

paragraphs: Your La Sportiva Skwama (2.5 down) is typical for La Sportiva; your La Sportiva Finale (3 down) is rather aggressive for La Sportiva; your La Sportiva Solution (2.5 down) is typical for La Sportiva; your Scarpa Instinct VSR (1.5 down) is typical for Scarpa.
Your La Sportiva Finale's squeezed toes is most likely a sizing problem, you could try a size larger for a better fit.

Your La Sportiva Solution's heel feels tight while the other shoes fit on the heel. Your La Sportiva Solution's wide volume matches your wide heel width, but your deep heel may not fit cups designed for less backward projection. The cause is likely heel depth, so try shoes with a deeper, more sculpted heel cup.
In 2 of your 4 shoes (La Sportiva Solution and Scarpa Instinct VSR), your toes feel squeezed because the toe form doesn't match your Roman toes. Look for a Roman-compatible toe box.
interpretation.title: What Your Current Shoe Fit Tells Us

paragraphs: Based on your scan and current shoe fit, we target shoes with Roman toe form, normal forefoot width, and wide heel width.

Given your preference for trad and multipitch outdoors on mixed and a comfort fit, we prioritize very stiff lace-ups with flat profile and no asymmetry, with ankle protection for trad/multipitch wear.
The softer, stiffer, and budget tiers below offer alternative options around this target.
interpretation.title: What to Look For

recommendations.description: Lace-up with Roman toe form, medium fit throughout. 4.5mm FS-Quattro rubber with a full, hard midsole, resulting in a firm shoe with flat profile and symmetric profile.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Tradeoff: Slightly tighter in the heel than we target.

recommendations.tradeoffs: Tradeoff: Slightly tighter in the heel and slightly more asymmetric than we target.
